question_text: Two waves are compared: Wave A has a frequency of 20 Hz and travels at 40 m/s; Wave B has a frequency of 10 Hz and travels at 80 m/s. Which wave has the longer wavelength, and by how much?
correct_answer: Wave B has the longer wavelength by 2 m. Wave A: 40 ÷ 20 = 2 m. Wave B: 80 ÷ 10 = 8 m. Difference: 8 - 2 = 6 m.
explanation: Calculate each wavelength using wavelength = speed ÷ frequency. Wave A: 40 ÷ 20 = 2 m. Wave B: 80 ÷ 10 = 8 m. Wave B is longer by 6 m. Even though Wave B has half the frequency, its much higher speed means it has a longer wavelength.
